findings.title: wavToPcm loads entire WAV file into memory with no size cap
findings.issue: `wavToPcm(file)` calls `file.readBytes()` which materialises the whole file as a `ByteArray` before the header is inspected. A poisoned Room row (future migration bug, or a hand-edited DB on a rooted device) could point at a multi-hundred-MB file that legitimately lives under `filesDir/attachments/` (passed the containment check) and trigger an OutOfMemoryError on any Persistent chat bootstrap. The check `dataSize > bytes.size - 44` only runs AFTER the allocation.
findings.impact: Local DoS: crashing the app on chat open. Not an arbitrary-file-read vector because containment check runs first at path resolution; purely an availability concern. Minor given the threat model (device-local, attacker has write access to app storage = already compromised).
findings.remediation: Cheap mitigation: before `file.readBytes()`, early-return when `file.length() > MAX_WAV_BYTES` (e.g. 50 MB — well above any realistic phone-captured mono PCM recording), logging to `attachment-read`. Same idea for `decodeImageFromRelativePath` in `ChatViewModel` — use `BitmapFactory.Options(inJustDecodeBounds = true)` first and bail when `outWidth * outHeight * 4` exceeds a threshold.

findings.title: Containment check allows absolute path in imagePath/audioPath but relies on canonical comparison
findings.issue: `File(filesDir, relativePath)` silently discards `filesDir` when `relativePath` is absolute (Java `File(File, String)` contract). For a poisoned row with `imagePath = "/data/data/app.sanctum.machina/databases/sanctum.db"`, the resolved candidate canonicalises to that absolute path and is correctly rejected by the `startsWith(attachmentsRoot + File.separator)` check — so the current code IS safe. However, the method name and contract suggest the caller expects a relative path; accepting absolute input and relying on the containment check as the sole guard means a future reviewer who tweaks the check (e.g. normalises via `relativePath.removePrefix("/")` thinking it's a safety improvement) could introduce a traversal regression.
findings.impact: Latent hardening gap — not exploitable today, but the control is one edit away from breaking. The in-tree test `persistentMode_imagePathOutsideAttachmentsRoot_throwsAndOmits` only covers `..` traversal, not absolute-path poisoning.
findings.remediation: Fail fast on non-relative input: `require(!File(relativePath).isAbsolute) { "relative path required" }` at the top of `resolveInsideAttachmentsRoot`. Also add a test case with `imagePath = "/etc/passwd"` (or the Android-analog `/data/system/...`) to the `ChatViewModelTest` containment suite so the invariant is pinned.

findings.title: errorLog.e logs relative attachment paths for every decode failure
findings.issue: Per task 18 B2, `errors.log` must not contain user-sensitive data. The new `attachment-read` sites log the raw `imagePath`/`audioPath` string (e.g. `"image decode failed: attachments/7/img_<uuid>.png"`) and, for the missing-file / null-bitmap cases, the full `file.absolutePath`. The filename itself is a random UUID produced by the app (not user-chosen / not PII), and the absolute path reveals only the app's private-storage layout — so in the current scheme there is no chat-text or user-identifiable content leaking. This is borderline: the paths contain a chat-id integer which correlates log lines to specific conversations, and if the log-export UX ever ships attachments alongside logs, the path becomes more sensitive.
findings.impact: Low. An exported log lets a support engineer see how many failed reads a given chat had and the chat_id that owns them, but not the chat content.
findings.remediation: Either (a) redact the chat-id from logged paths — log only the leaf filename `file.name` — or (b) document explicitly in `ErrorLog.kt` that paths under `attachments/` are considered non-sensitive. Not blocking for the commit.

findings.title: Unbounded launch-per-decode-failure on errorLog writes
findings.issue: `decodeAttachmentsForEntity` is called for every Room row on every `observeByChat` emission; while `attachmentCache` memoises successful decodes, a row that FAILS decode (missing file, SecurityException) is NOT cached — the failure path returns `listOf()` and the cache stores that empty list only because the overall `attachmentCache[entity.id] = decoded` happens unconditionally in `toDomainMessageWithAttachments`. Good for successful attachments. However, inside `decodeImageFromRelativePath`, a failing file produces `viewModelScope.launch { errorLog.e(...) }` on every call — but the call is skipped on cache-hit, so in practice you get one launch per row per VM lifecycle. The risk is subtle: the containment-check `SecurityException` is thrown AT path resolution, caught by `runCatching` inside `decodeAttachmentsForEntity`, which logs ONCE — that's correct — but the cache still stores the empty list so the decode is NOT retried on subsequent emissions. OK. The actual residual concern is that `launch` without a Dispatcher inherits the ViewModel's main-immediate context; many simultaneous failures (10-msg batch) dispatch 10 coroutines competing on the ErrorLog mutex.
findings.impact: Negligible on real devices (small N, mutex throughput is microseconds per write). Flagging for completeness.
findings.remediation: No change required. If a perf issue ever materialises, batch the failure logs into a single `errorLog.e("attachment-read", "decode failed for $N attachments in chat=$chatId", firstCause)`.

notes.b1_containment: Confirmed: resolveInsideAttachmentsRoot canonicalises via File.canonicalFile (follows symlinks, resolves `..`) and requires candidate.path to start with attachmentsRoot.path + File.separator. Absolute-path poisoning is rejected because the resolved candidate does not live under attachmentsRoot. Symlink escape is rejected because canonicalFile resolves link targets BEFORE the startsWith check. Test `persistentMode_imagePathOutsideAttachmentsRoot_throwsAndOmits` covers `..` traversal. Verdict: safe.
notes.b1_exception_leakage: BitmapFactory failure returns null (not a throwable); the null branch logs `attachment-read` with no user-surface exposure — the attachment is silently omitted and the text message renders. SecurityException and wavToPcm IOExceptions are caught by runCatching inside decodeAttachmentsForEntity; the cause is forwarded to errorLog but never shown in the UI. Verdict: safe.
notes.b1_poisoned_row: A future migration bug could write arbitrary strings into imagePath/audioPath. Even so, the containment check canonicalises to real filesystem paths and rejects anything outside attachments/. Verdict: safe.
notes.wavtopcm_header_validation: RIFF + WAVE magic checked; dataSize bound-checked against bytes.size - 44 before copyOfRange; denom=0 guarded before division; sampleRate/bits/channels non-positive rejected. copyOfRange cannot OOB. Size-cap on file.readBytes is the one remaining DoS surface — filed as sa-18-1.
notes.b2_trace_logging: Grepped all new errorLog.e sites in WarmupCoordinator, DefaultModelRegistry, ChatViewModel: none log user chat text, user message content, or PII. history-read logs `id=${id.id}` (chat_id only, an integer). attachment-read logs random-UUID filenames and absolute paths to app-private storage. registry logs model names and init error strings (engine-emitted). Verdict: B2 privacy invariant holds, with the caveat in sa-18-3.
notes.b4_resume: autoResumeTarget.text originates from MessageEntity.text (already-persisted user input, sanitized at the original send-time code path). Flowing it through resumePendingAssistantPersistent → dispatchInferencePersistent is the same trust path as a fresh USER send. No new untrusted-data ingress. Verdict: safe.
notes.b5_routes: Routes "model_manager" and "about" match existing Phase-3 NavGraph entries; grep confirms both targets exist. onOpenModelManager and onNavigateToAbout callbacks are distinct lambdas wired separately — no confused-deputy between them. Verdict: safe.
notes.owasp_sweep: A01 access-control: containment check is the sole access control on attachment reads; functioning correctly. A02 crypto: no new crypto in this commit. A03 injection: no new SQL/string concatenation — MessageDao.lastByChat uses parameterised :chatId binding. A05 misconfig: removed dead IconButton + string — no net new surface. A06 deps: no dependency version changes in this commit. A07 authn: no auth surfaces touched. A08 integrity: MessageEntity readback is trusted Room data; no deserialization of external blobs. A09 logging: see sa-18-3. A10 SSRF: no outbound HTTP in this commit.
